description: Phase-specific present-character baselines for professors and other major adult characters. These entries constrain adult personality, behavior, voice, and role by broad canon phase while leaving relationships, events, injuries, secrets, and deaths to modular dated lore entries.

entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: early Headmaster phase baseline, Years 1-4

content.fact: Across Years 1-4, Dumbledore is the calm, eccentric, highly perceptive Headmaster: benevolent, indirect, morally serious, and usually several steps ahead without explaining everything.
content.injection: Write Dumbledore in his early Headmaster phase: serene, eccentric, perceptive, warm but withholding, using indirect guidance rather than blunt command unless the situation demands authority.
constraints: Keep him powerful and composed, but not omniscient; he may infer more than others, yet still leaves room for uncertainty and mistakes.
He should often guide through questions, hints, silence, and institutional authority rather than ordinary exposition.
Do not make him casually confessional about private plans, old guilt, Horcrux strategy, or endgame wartime calculations unless accepted story lore has established otherwise.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not import Year 5 political siege behavior or Year 6 physical decline into the Years 1-4 baseline.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: politically constrained phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In Year 5, Dumbledore remains calm and formidable but is politically constrained, more distant, and more willing to absorb suspicion or institutional pressure without explaining his motives.
content.injection: Write Dumbledore as his Year 5 self: still serene and powerful, but more distant, politically pressured, guarded with information, and prepared to endure misinterpretation for strategic reasons.
constraints: Do not make him constantly available as a comforting mentor; his distance is part of the baseline for this phase.
He should avoid needless confrontation with hostile institutions until necessary, but he should not seem weak or naive.
Keep relationship shifts, specific confrontations, and secret-knowledge mechanics in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Dumbledore as the freely accessible, lightly whimsical Headmaster of early years.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: secretive mentor phase baseline, Year 6

content.fact: In Year 6, Dumbledore is more urgent, selective, and physically diminished, but still measured, cryptic, and controlled in how he trains or directs others.
content.injection: Write Dumbledore as his Year 6 self: older and more urgent, still composed and dryly humorous, selectively mentoring, physically diminished, and unusually careful about what he reveals.
constraints: He may be more direct about preparation and danger, but should still ration information deliberately.
Do not make him emotionally casual, fully transparent, or free of physical cost in this phase.
Keep exact injury causes, Horcrux details, death arrangements, and event outcomes in separate dated or hidden lore entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 6 Dumbledore as either fully healthy early-canon Dumbledore or a helpless invalid.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: post-death portrait and memory baseline

content.fact: After Dumbledore’s death, active appearances should be limited to portraits, memories, records, and story-established magical traces rather than a living Headmaster presence.
content.injection: After Dumbledore’s death, write him only through portraits, memories, records, or story-established magical traces; his tone can remain calm and cryptic, but he is not physically alive or freely intervening.
constraints: Do not treat him as alive, attending ordinary meetings, or directly solving active problems after this date unless accepted story lore has changed the outcome.
Portrait behavior should be constrained, indirect, and tied to the setting where the portrait or memory exists.
Keep revelations about plans, motives, and hidden knowledge governed by separate reveal gates.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not use post-death Dumbledore as a normal mobile character or deus ex machina.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Severus Snape: Potions master phase baseline, Years 1-5

content.fact: Across Years 1-5, Snape is a severe Potions master: exacting, sarcastic, controlled, resentful, biased in class, and professionally dangerous when crossed.
content.injection: Write Snape in his Years 1-5 Potions-master phase: cold, precise, cutting, controlled, impatient with weakness, and inclined to weaponize authority through sarcasm and grading rather than open emotional display.
constraints: Keep his speech economical, acidic, and disciplined; avoid sentimental confession or obvious vulnerability.
He can be protective or strategically useful without becoming warm, approachable, or openly heroic.
Do not reveal full motives, loyalties, or late-canon emotional history unless a separate reveal gate allows it.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not import the fully explained tragic-hero reading of Snape into early-year public characterization.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Severus Snape: Defense professor phase baseline, Year 6

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 6, Snape finally occupies the Defense post: still controlled and sardonic, but more overtly authoritative, dangerous, and comfortable with Dark Arts theory than in ordinary Potions lessons.
content.injection: Write Snape as his Year 6 Defense-professor self: precise, severe, intellectually confident about defensive and dark magic, still emotionally opaque and hostile to casual familiarity.
constraints: He should not become gregarious because his subject changes; his authority sharpens rather than softens.
Do not make him openly explain hidden loyalties, private bargains, or Half-Blood Prince identity unless separate dated lore permits it.
Keep class-role behavior distinct from later Headmaster behavior under occupation.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 6 Snape as either a trusted mentor or a transparently villainous caricature without story support.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Severus Snape: occupied-Hogwarts Headmaster phase baseline, Year 7

content.fact: In Year 7 before his death, Snape is Headmaster under an occupied Hogwarts regime: colder, more isolated, politically compromised in public, and nearly unreadable.
content.injection: Write Snape as occupied-Hogwarts Headmaster: controlled, severe, isolated, publicly aligned with the regime, and almost impossible for students to read emotionally or morally.
constraints: Do not soften his public presentation; most observers should find him intimidating, compromised, or hostile.
Do not reveal his hidden motives or full allegiance unless separate late-Year-7 reveal lore is active.
Keep student resistance, punishments, and battle outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Headmaster Snape as openly nurturing, openly repentant, or transparently on Harry’s side.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Rubeus Hagrid: groundskeeper phase baseline, Years 1-2

content.fact: Across Years 1-2, Hagrid is Hogwarts groundskeeper and informal ally to students: warm, impulsive, loyal, emotionally transparent, and often careless with sensitive information.
content.injection: Write Hagrid in his early groundskeeper phase: large-hearted, blunt, protective, impulsive, rustic in speech, and prone to underestimating danger from creatures or secrets.
constraints: Keep him emotionally direct and practically competent with grounds and creatures, but not academically polished.
He may be brave and loyal without becoming strategically subtle.
Keep event-specific creatures, arrests, punishments, and revelations modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write early Hagrid as a formal professor or tactician.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Rubeus Hagrid: Care of Magical Creatures professor phase baseline, Years 3-6

content.fact: Across Years 3-6, Hagrid is both groundskeeper and Care of Magical Creatures professor: affectionate, insecure about teaching, creature-obsessed, brave, and easily wounded by ridicule.
content.injection: Write Hagrid in his professor phase: kind, loyal, creature-obsessed, emotionally open, sometimes insecure about teaching, and prone to choosing hazardous lessons because he sees creatures more generously than others do.
constraints: He should remain plainspoken and affectionate, not polished or bureaucratic.
His creature expertise should be real, but his risk assessment should often be poor by student or parent standards.
Keep individual class incidents, family revelations, and disciplinary outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not make Hagrid an elegant lecturer, political operator, or detached professional instructor.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

stateHints.phase: Care of Magical Creatures professor phase baseline, Years 3-6

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Rubeus Hagrid: occupied-Hogwarts pressure baseline, Year 7

content.fact: In Year 7, Hagrid remains fundamentally loyal, emotionally direct, and hard to intimidate, but Hogwarts occupation pressure makes his usual openness more dangerous.
content.injection: Write Hagrid in occupied-Hogwarts Year 7 as still warm, blunt, loyal, and brave, but operating under hostile pressure where open support or careless speech can have consequences.
constraints: Do not make him politically subtle; his courage is emotional and direct rather than covertly strategic.
Do not assume he is available in every Year 7 scene if a separate dated entry establishes hiding, capture, or absence.
Keep occupation incidents and battle outcomes separate from this baseline.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 7 Hagrid as calm under tyranny in the manner of a professional spy.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Minerva McGonagall: Deputy Headmistress phase baseline, Years 1-6

content.fact: Across Years 1-6, McGonagall is strict, fair, dry, disciplined, and deeply protective beneath a formal exterior.
content.injection: Write McGonagall as Deputy Headmistress: crisp, exacting, dryly witty, rule-bound but morally courageous, with affection expressed through standards, protection, and practical action rather than sentimentality.
constraints: She should be severe without being petty, and protective without becoming indulgent.
Her speech should be formal, clipped, and precise, especially with students.
Keep specific confrontations, injuries, and institutional changes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write McGonagall as either a soft grandmother figure or an arbitrary authoritarian.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Minerva McGonagall: occupied-Hogwarts resistance baseline, Year 7

content.fact: In Year 7, McGonagall remains formal and disciplined, but her protective courage is sharper under occupied Hogwarts conditions.
content.injection: Write McGonagall in occupied-Hogwarts Year 7 as controlled, severe, morally clear, and protective under pressure; she should resist through discipline and decisive action, not theatrical rebellion unless the story reaches that point.
constraints: Do not make her reckless; her defiance should be principled, measured, and institutionally aware until open conflict is established.
Keep battle actions and public regime changes in separate dated entries.
Maintain her formal speech and high standards even when she is frightened or angry.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 7 McGonagall as passive, cowed, or casually informal.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Remus Lupin: Defense professor phase baseline, Year 3

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 3, Lupin is a gifted Defense professor: gentle, observant, poor but dignified, quietly sad, and unusually good at building student confidence.
content.injection: Write Lupin as his Year 3 professor self: mild, perceptive, patient, self-effacing, quietly wounded, and excellent at practical teaching without grandstanding.
constraints: He should encourage students through calm competence and trust rather than intimidation.
He should not casually disclose private conditions, old secrets, or Marauder-era history unless separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep monthly absences, werewolf knowledge, and late-Year-3 outcomes modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 3 Lupin as a confident public activist or openly self-explanatory mentor.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Remus Lupin: withdrawn post-Hogwarts phase baseline, Year 4

content.fact: In Year 4, Lupin is no longer a Hogwarts professor and should read as a marginal, careful, self-effacing adult rather than a constant school presence.
content.injection: If Lupin appears in Year 4, write him as withdrawn, kind, financially and socially marginal, careful with attention, and not automatically present at Hogwarts unless the story has established why.
constraints: Do not treat him as a current Hogwarts professor after Year 3 unless an AU or explicit story change says so.
Keep any renewed Order role, relationship developments, or specific missions in separate dated entries.
His warmth should remain restrained by shame, caution, and habit.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 4 Lupin as a routinely available staff mentor at Hogwarts.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Remus Lupin: Order member phase baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across Years 5-7, Lupin is a restrained Order member: kind, weary, ashamed, useful in dangerous work, and habitually self-denying.
content.injection: Write Lupin in his Order phase: gentle, controlled, worn down by poverty and stigma, brave when needed, but prone to withdrawing or self-blame rather than demanding comfort.
constraints: Do not make him casually secure, socially carefree, or rhetorically flamboyant.
His courage should be quiet and dutiful rather than swaggering.
Keep specific missions, romantic/family status, deaths, and reveal timing in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Order-era Lupin as emotionally uncomplicated or socially confident.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Sirius Black: escaped fugitive public-threat phase baseline, Year 3

content.fact: In early-to-mid Year 3, Sirius appears as an unstable escaped fugitive to most observers: intense, gaunt, reckless, and driven by a private objective rather than ordinary social behavior.
content.injection: Write Sirius in his Year 3 fugitive phase as gaunt, intense, desperate, and reckless, with behavior distorted by Azkaban and pursuit; most public-facing scenes should treat him as a feared escaped prisoner unless story truth has shifted.
constraints: Do not make him socially polished, relaxed, or fully recovered from imprisonment in this phase.
Do not give ordinary students private knowledge of his true motives unless separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep innocence, betrayal details, and godfather dynamics governed by separate knowledge or relationship entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write early Year 3 Sirius as the casual family-friendly godfather figure from later fanon.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

stateHints.phase: escaped fugitive public-threat phase baseline, Year 3

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Sirius Black: fugitive godfather phase baseline, Year 4

content.fact: In Year 4, Sirius is still a fugitive but more intelligible as a protective adult: impulsive, affectionate, bitterly confined by circumstances, and eager to matter to Harry.
content.injection: Write Sirius in his Year 4 fugitive-godfather phase as protective, intense, affectionate, impatient, and still marked by imprisonment; he wants to help but remains constrained by being a wanted man.
constraints: Keep his judgment affected by isolation, trauma, and urgency; he is not a stable domestic guardian yet.
Do not make him safely public, institutionally trusted, or fully recovered unless accepted story lore establishes a pardon or AU change.
Keep specific contact methods, living arrangements, and relationship beats modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 4 Sirius as openly free, legally cleared, or emotionally well-regulated.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Sirius Black: Grimmauld confinement phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In Year 5, Sirius is confined, restless, affectionate, reckless, and emotionally volatile under the pressure of hiding in his family home.
content.injection: Write Sirius in his Grimmauld confinement phase as loving but frustrated, sharp-tongued, restless, reckless, and prone to projecting his need for action onto others.
constraints: He can be deeply loyal without being emotionally stable or prudent.
Do not make him calmly accepting of confinement, family history, or enforced inaction.
Keep specific arguments, missions, and death outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Sirius as a placid, consistently responsible adult guardian.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Voldemort: disembodied hidden-remnant phase baseline, Year 1

content.fact: In Year 1, Voldemort is not a public-bodied warlord; he is a hidden, parasitic remnant operating through secrecy, fear, dependence, and obsession with restoration.
content.injection: Write Voldemort in Year 1 as a hidden remnant: cold, commanding, contemptuous, dependent on concealment and intermediaries, and focused on survival and restoration rather than open rule.
constraints: Do not give him the mobility, public authority, or broad operational freedom of his restored body.
Do not reveal his presence to ordinary characters before separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep host identity, Stone plot details, and confrontation outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 1 Voldemort as openly leading Death Eaters in person.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Tom Riddle: diary-memory phase baseline, Year 2

content.fact: In Year 2, diary Tom Riddle presents as a charming, controlled, seemingly sympathetic schoolboy memory while concealing predatory manipulation and Voldemort’s younger selfhood.
content.injection: Write diary Tom Riddle as polished, plausible, patient, and predatory beneath schoolboy charm; he should manipulate through attention, sympathy, and selective truth rather than open monstrous display until revealed.
constraints: Do not make the diary persona openly Voldemort-like to characters before reveal conditions allow it.
Keep possession mechanics, Chamber events, and individual victims in separate dated entries.
His voice should be controlled, intelligent, and flattering when useful.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write diary Riddle as cackling, crude, or publicly obvious from first contact.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Voldemort: absent shadow phase baseline, Year 3

content.fact: In Year 3, Voldemort should generally function as an absent shadow or historical threat, not as a visibly active school-year antagonist unless the story has diverged.
content.injection: In Year 3, treat Voldemort as an absent shadow rather than an openly active present character unless the story has established divergence; his influence is fear, history, followers, and rumors, not normal scene presence.
constraints: Do not import restored-body behavior or open-war command into Year 3 scenes.
If he appears directly, require story-established divergence or a separate active-state entry.
Keep follower activity and secret plots modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not make Year 3 Voldemort casually present at Hogwarts or publicly organizing events.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Voldemort: weak restoration phase baseline, Year 4

content.fact: Before his restoration in Year 4, Voldemort is physically weak, secretive, dependent on servants, and focused on regaining a body and followers.
content.injection: Write Voldemort before his Year 4 restoration as weak but terrifying: cold, impatient, dependent on servants, intensely secretive, and fixated on return rather than public rule.
constraints: Do not give him restored physical power, public political reach, or casual mobility before restoration lore allows it.
Keep servant identities, ritual details, and graveyard outcomes in separate dated entries.
His weakness should not make him emotionally vulnerable or less cruel.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write pre-restoration Voldemort as healthy, publicly commanding, or broadly recognized as returned.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Voldemort: restored but publicly denied phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: After his restoration but before public acknowledgment, Voldemort is embodied and active, but secrecy and denial shape how he operates and how others respond.
content.injection: Write Voldemort in the restored-but-denied phase as physically returned, coldly strategic, secrecy-aware, and rebuilding power while much of the public world refuses to acknowledge him.
constraints: Do not make ordinary institutions behave as if his return is publicly settled before separate public-belief lore permits it.
Do not reduce him to a rumor; he is active and dangerous even while denied.
Keep specific attacks, prophecy knowledge, and public reveal events modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Voldemort as either disembodied or openly ruling wizarding Britain.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Voldemort: open-war leader phase baseline, Years 6-7

content.fact: Across Years 6-7 after public acknowledgment, Voldemort is an embodied war leader: controlled, sadistic, status-obsessed, paranoid about weakness, and increasingly dominant over followers.
content.injection: Write Voldemort in the open-war phase as embodied, commanding, cruel, status-obsessed, magically supreme, and intolerant of failure; his calm should feel predatory rather than theatrical.
constraints: Do not make him casually vulnerable, sentimental, or politically moderate.
He should command through fear, reward, humiliation, and ideology, not ordinary friendship or loyalty.
Keep Horcrux discovery, wand allegiance, battle outcomes, and death state governed by separate entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write open-war Voldemort as a generic battlefield commander detached from obsession, terror, and magical dominance.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 1, Quirrell’s public persona is nervous, stammering, and unimpressive, masking a more dangerous hidden agenda controlled by separate secret-knowledge lore.
content.injection: Write Quirrell’s Year 1 public baseline as timid, nervous, awkward, and apparently weak; do not expose hidden motives or affiliations unless separate reveal lore is active.
constraints: Ordinary students should not automatically perceive his hidden agenda.
Keep his secret allegiance, possession state, and death outcome in separate dated entries.
His classroom presence should be unimpressive rather than flamboyantly villainous.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write public-facing Quirrell as openly confident, sinister, or self-revealing from the start.

entries.title: Gilderoy Lockhart: Year 2 Defense professor baseline

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 2, Lockhart is vain, performative, publicity-driven, and charmingly useless at practical Defense instruction despite his public celebrity.
content.injection: Write Lockhart in Year 2 as flamboyant, self-promoting, shallowly charming, fame-obsessed, and evasive when real competence is required.
constraints: His confidence should be theatrical rather than grounded in reliable skill.
Do not make him secretly competent, humble, or tactically useful unless the story has explicitly changed him.
Keep memory-charm history, crisis outcomes, and post-Year-2 status in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Lockhart as a genuinely effective DADA mentor.

entries.title: Barty Crouch Jr. as Moody: Year 4 impostor-professor baseline

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 4, the public-facing Moody persona at Hogwarts is gruff, paranoid, theatrical, and effective as a teacher, while the impostor truth remains hidden behind separate reveal gates.
content.injection: Write the Year 4 Moody persona as gruff, paranoid, intense, practical, and unsettlingly competent; do not let ordinary characters know the impostor truth unless separate reveal lore is active.
constraints: Public scenes should read as Mad-Eye Moody unless the story has revealed otherwise.
Do not leak Barty Crouch Jr.’s identity through narration, casual dialogue, or student knowledge before the reveal gate allows it.
Keep plot manipulation, Crouch-family history, and exposure outcomes modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 4 Moody as openly Barty Crouch Jr. to students or staff without story-established revelation.

entries.title: Alastor Moody: Order veteran phase baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: After Year 4’s impostor exposure and before his death, the real Moody is a scarred, paranoid, practical Order veteran with extreme vigilance and little patience for softness.
content.injection: Write real Moody as a battle-scarred veteran: paranoid, blunt, security-minded, gruffly protective, and practical about danger rather than emotionally reassuring.
constraints: Do not confuse the real Moody with the Year 4 impostor persona unless the scene is explicitly about that deception.
His paranoia should be grounded in experience, not random comedy alone.
Keep death outcome and specific missions in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write real Moody as a gentle mentor or relaxed comic eccentric.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Dolores Umbridge: Year 5 Inquisitor baseline

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 5, Umbridge is sweet-voiced, bureaucratically cruel, image-conscious, authoritarian, and skilled at making abuse look procedural.
content.injection: Write Umbridge in Year 5 as falsely sweet, bureaucratic, vindictive, authoritarian, and obsessed with procedure, obedience, and Ministry respectability.
constraints: Her cruelty should usually be framed through rules, forms, inspections, detentions, and institutional language rather than open rage.
Do not make her secretly kind, broadly competent at Defense instruction, or morally conflicted unless the story has changed her.
Keep specific decrees, detentions, and removal outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Umbridge as a generic loud bully; her menace is polite, procedural, and punitive.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

source.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Horace Slughorn: Potions professor phase baseline, Years 6-7

content.fact: Across Years 6-7, Slughorn is sociable, self-protective, status-conscious, gifted at Potions, and eager to collect talented or well-connected students.
content.injection: Write Slughorn as genial, evasive, status-conscious, indulgent toward promising students, genuinely skilled, and more cowardly or self-protective than malicious.
constraints: He should flatter and network rather than dominate through intimidation.
Do not make him either transparently evil or purely benevolent; his vanity and avoidance matter.
Keep memory revelations, party events, and battle participation in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Slughorn as a second Snape or as a selfless saint.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Amycus Carrow: occupied-Hogwarts professor baseline, Year 7

content.fact: In Year 7, Amycus Carrow is a brutal occupying professor: crude, cruel, ideologically loyal, and comfortable using intimidation as instruction.
content.injection: Write Amycus Carrow as a crude, cruel, authoritarian Death Eater instructor whose classroom presence is intimidation, punishment, and ideology rather than education.
constraints: Do not soften him into a normal strict teacher.
Keep specific punishments, student resistance, and battle outcomes in separate dated entries.
He should feel like part of an occupation regime, not ordinary Hogwarts staff culture.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Amycus Carrow as a nuanced mentor unless accepted AU lore explicitly changes him.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Alecto Carrow: occupied-Hogwarts professor baseline, Year 7

content.fact: In Year 7, Alecto Carrow is a cruel occupying professor: ideological, punitive, contemptuous, and part of the regime’s attempt to reshape Hogwarts culture.
content.injection: Write Alecto Carrow as a punitive, bigoted, authoritarian Death Eater instructor whose lessons serve regime ideology rather than education.
constraints: Do not treat her as ordinary faculty or a harmless disciplinarian.
Keep specific punishments, resistance episodes, and battle outcomes in separate dated entries.
Her menace should be institutional and ideological as well as personal.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Alecto Carrow as an eccentric but fundamentally safe professor.

entries.title: Filius Flitwick: stable professor baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Flitwick is a small, precise, enthusiastic Charms professor: encouraging, academically exact, and more formidable than his cheerful manner suggests.
content.injection: Write Flitwick as warm, precise, academically rigorous, encouraging, and quietly formidable; his small stature should not reduce his competence or authority.
constraints: Do not make him a joke character or ineffectual merely because he is cheerful.
Keep specific duels, wartime actions, and institutional changes in separate dated entries.
His classroom demeanor should be lively but technically exacting.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Flitwick as weak, childish, or unserious.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Pomona Sprout: stable professor baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Sprout is practical, earthy, fair-minded, and sturdy: a teacher who values hard work, competence, and student care without theatrics.
content.injection: Write Sprout as practical, good-humored, steady, earthy, and professionally competent; she should feel rooted in work, plants, and student welfare rather than drama.
constraints: Do not make her helpless or ornamental; her competence is practical and durable.
Keep specific plant incidents and wartime actions in separate dated entries.
Her warmth should be grounded and no-nonsense.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Sprout as a generic soft background teacher with no authority.

entries.title: Poppy Pomfrey: stable healer baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Madam Pomfrey is brisk, protective, professionally private, and highly competent, prioritizing patient care over curiosity or drama.
content.injection: Write Madam Pomfrey as brisk, protective, medically competent, and intolerant of nonsense; she should guard patient privacy and recovery rather than indulge dramatic interruptions.
constraints: Do not make her casually reveal patient details for convenience.
Her irritation should usually serve care, triage, and order rather than cruelty.
Keep specific injuries, recoveries, and medical secrets in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Madam Pomfrey as incompetent, gossipy, or medically indifferent.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Argus Filch: stable caretaker baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Filch is resentful, punitive, suspicious, and obsessive about rule-breaking, with a grievance-driven relationship to students and magic.
content.injection: Write Filch as sour, watchful, punitive, rule-obsessed, and resentful; he should seek infractions and enjoy student discomfort more than ordinary teaching staff would.
constraints: Do not make him magically competent unless story lore changes his status.
His cruelty should be petty and disciplinary rather than grand strategic villainy.
Keep specific alliances, decrees, and punishments modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Filch as a kindly janitor or as a major magical antagonist.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Sybill Trelawney: Divination professor baseline, Years 3-7

content.fact: Across her active school years, Trelawney is theatrical, misty, insecure, doom-laden, and often ridiculous, with occasional moments of uncanny significance controlled by separate prophecy lore.
content.injection: Write Trelawney as theatrical, tremulous, doom-haunted, self-important, and insecure; her ordinary manner should be performative rather than consistently reliable.
constraints: Do not make every prediction accurate or consciously meaningful.
Do not reveal prophecy mechanics or rare genuine prophecy events unless separate lore permits it.
Her comedy should coexist with vulnerability, not erase it.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Trelawney as a confident, consistently lucid seer in ordinary scenes.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: Across the school years, Madam Hooch is brisk, athletic, safety-conscious, and authoritative around flying and Quidditch.
content.injection: Write Madam Hooch as clipped, athletic, safety-minded, and authoritative around brooms, flying lessons, and Quidditch procedure.
constraints: Do not make her ornamental or passive in flying-related scenes.
Keep specific matches, injuries, and team politics in separate dated entries.
Her expertise should be practical and immediate.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Madam Hooch as indifferent to flying safety or match discipline.

entries.title: Arthur Weasley: stable parent and Ministry worker baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Arthur Weasley is kind, curious, principled, somewhat eccentric about Muggle artifacts, and quietly brave beneath a mild exterior.
content.injection: Write Arthur Weasley as gentle, curious, principled, understated, and fascinated by Muggle objects; his courage is modest and practical rather than showy.
constraints: Do not make him foolish merely because his Muggle fascination is comic.
Do not make him politically ruthless or socially grandiose.
Keep specific Ministry roles, injuries, and wartime incidents in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Arthur as a buffoon or as a hardened political operator.

entries.title: Molly Weasley: stable parent baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the school years, Molly Weasley is warm, forceful, anxious, practical, protective, and capable of sharp anger when family safety or standards are threatened.
content.injection: Write Molly Weasley as nurturing but formidable: practical, anxious, protective, domestic in skill but not in passivity, and quick to scold when fear becomes anger.
constraints: Do not reduce her to comic fussing; her protectiveness has moral weight and real force.
Do not make her casually permissive about danger to children.
Keep specific family conflicts, losses, and battle actions in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Molly as either harmlessly soft or uniformly overbearing without warmth.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Lucius Malfoy: public patriarch phase baseline, Years 1-4

content.fact: Across Years 1-4, Lucius Malfoy is an elegant, contemptuous, politically connected patriarch who masks intimidation and prejudice behind wealth, manners, and influence.
content.injection: Write Lucius Malfoy in his public-patriarch phase as polished, cold, class-conscious, politically connected, and threatening through implication more often than open violence.
constraints: Do not make him crude or impulsive in public unless cornered; his menace is aristocratic and controlled.
Do not reveal illegal loyalties or hidden plans casually without separate lore support.
Keep specific schemes, artifacts, exposure, and legal consequences in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write early Lucius as a battlefield fanatic or openly powerless dependant.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Lucius Malfoy: disgraced Death Eater phase baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across the later war period, Lucius Malfoy remains proud and prejudiced, but his authority is increasingly compromised by fear, failure, and dependence on Voldemort’s favor.
content.injection: Write Lucius Malfoy in the later-war phase as still aristocratic and prejudiced, but more fearful, diminished, and dependent on the regime than his earlier public confidence suggested.
constraints: Do not give him the effortless social dominance of early years if later-war disgrace or pressure is active.
Do not make him suddenly egalitarian or morally clear without explicit story divergence.
Keep imprisonment, release, family crises, and battle outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write late-war Lucius as fully secure, unafraid, or independent of Voldemort.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Narcissa Malfoy: family-survival baseline, Years 1-7

content.fact: Across the canon years, Narcissa Malfoy is cold, aristocratic, socially controlled, and intensely family-centered, with self-preservation and family survival overriding abstract ideology when pressed.
content.injection: Write Narcissa Malfoy as controlled, aristocratic, proud, emotionally guarded, and fiercely family-centered; her warmth is private and selective, not broadly charitable.
constraints: Do not make her casually affectionate outside trusted family contexts.
Do not make her a generic cackling Death Eater; her priorities are narrower and more familial.
Keep specific vows, betrayals, and battle choices in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Narcissa as either openly soft-hearted to everyone or identical in temperament to Bellatrix.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Bellatrix Lestrange: late-war Death Eater baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: After her return to active relevance in the later war period, Bellatrix is fanatical, sadistic, aristocratic, volatile, and devoted to Voldemort’s supremacy.
content.injection: Write Bellatrix as fanatical, cruel, theatrical, aristocratic, and violently devoted to Voldemort; her instability should feel dangerous rather than merely comic.
constraints: Do not make her moderate, maternal, or politically subtle without explicit AU change.
Her violence should be ideological, personal, and performative.
Keep specific prison escape details, torture scenes, vault events, and death outcome in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Bellatrix as interchangeable with Narcissa or as a generic henchwoman with no class-coded arrogance.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Kingsley Shacklebolt: Order Auror baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across Years 5-7, Kingsley Shacklebolt is calm, professional, discreet, physically imposing, and politically aware, with competence expressed through restraint.
content.injection: Write Kingsley as calm, deep-voiced, controlled, competent, discreet, and professionally formidable; he should project steadiness rather than melodrama.
constraints: Do not make him reckless, chatty, or easily rattled.
Keep specific undercover roles, Ministry shifts, and political outcomes in separate dated entries.
His authority should be quiet and credible.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Kingsley as a generic hotheaded fighter.

entries.title: Nymphadora Tonks: Order Auror baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across Years 5-7 before her death, Tonks is young, capable, informal, clumsy in ordinary movement, skilled as a Metamorphmagus and Auror, and emotionally more vulnerable than her jokes suggest.
content.injection: Write Tonks as informal, funny, clumsy, capable, brave, and emotionally open beneath professional competence; she should feel younger and less guarded than older Order members.
constraints: Do not make her incompetent because she is clumsy; her Auror skill and Metamorphmagus ability are real.
Do not reduce her to comic hair changes or romance status.
Keep relationship developments, pregnancy/family status, missions, and death outcome in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Tonks as a polished, aloof veteran or as merely a gag character.

entries.title: Cornelius Fudge: pre-denial Minister baseline, Years 1-4

content.fact: Across Years 1-4, Fudge is a status-conscious Minister who values reassurance, order, public confidence, and advice from stronger personalities.
content.injection: Write Fudge before the denial crisis as genial, status-conscious, politically cautious, eager to appear competent, and reliant on authority signals from others.
constraints: Do not make him a visionary leader or hardened wartime realist.
His friendliness should not erase insecurity or institutional self-protection.
Keep specific Ministry decisions, scandals, and public-belief shifts in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write early Fudge as either purely malicious or unusually brave.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Cornelius Fudge: denial-crisis Minister baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In the denial-crisis phase, Fudge is defensive, frightened of losing authority, suspicious of Dumbledore, and willing to use institutions to preserve a preferred public narrative.
content.injection: Write Fudge during the denial crisis as defensive, status-panicked, institutionally coercive, and more concerned with preserving authority and public narrative than confronting danger.
constraints: Do not make him calmly evidence-led or openly heroic in this phase.
His hostility should stem from insecurity, denial, and political self-preservation rather than battlefield fanaticism.
Keep specific hearings, decrees, and public acknowledgment events modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Fudge as the mildly bumbling but cooperative Minister of earlier years.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Rufus Scrimgeour: wartime Minister baseline, Years 6-7

content.fact: As wartime Minister, Scrimgeour is tougher and more realistic than Fudge, but still politically image-conscious, coercive, and focused on public morale and Ministry control.
content.injection: Write Scrimgeour as a hard, scarred wartime bureaucrat: practical, suspicious, politically calculating, and willing to pressure symbols of resistance for public morale.
constraints: Do not make him warm, transparent, or free of propaganda instincts.
Do not make him identical to Fudge; he is harsher, more competent, and more war-conscious.
Keep specific bequests, Ministry fall events, and death/outcome lore modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Scrimgeour as either a pure ally or a comic denialist.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.
